---
title: "ModelSummaries.jl — Publication-Quality Regression Tables"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    geometry: margin=1in
    keep-tex: true
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
jupyter: julia-1.12
---

## Setup

```{julia}
using ModelSummaries
using SummaryTables: Concat, Superscript
using GLM, DataFrames, FixedEffectModels, RDatasets, CategoricalArrays
using Random; Random.seed!(42)
```

## Basic usage

`modelsummary` takes one or more regression models and returns a
`SummaryTables.Table` that renders natively to HTML, LaTeX, and Typst.

```{julia}
df = RDatasets.dataset("datasets", "iris")
df.isSmall = df.SepalWidth .< 2.9

m1 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth), df)
m2 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength), df)
m3 = lm(@formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + PetalWidth), df)

modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true)
```

## Groups, labels, and extra statistics

```{julia}
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3;
    stars  = true,
    groups = ["Parsimonious" "Parsimonious" "Full"],
    labels = Dict(
        "SepalWidth"  => "Sepal width",
        "PetalLength" => "Petal length",
        "PetalWidth"  => "Petal width",
    ),
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2, AdjR2, AIC],
)
```

## Fixed-effects models with checkmarks

Use `fe_indicator = "✓"` to replace the default `"Yes"`:

```{julia}
fe1 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + fe(Species)))
fe2 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + fe(Species)))
fe3 = reg(df, @formula(SepalLength ~ SepalWidth + PetalLength + fe(Species) + fe(isSmall)))

modelsummary(m1, fe1, fe2, fe3;
    stars        = true,
    fe_indicator = "✓",
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2],
)
```

## Estimator labels in the header

When columns use different estimators, pass `estimator_names` to get a
labelled header row. Consecutive equal names merge automatically.

```{julia}
modelsummary(m1, fe1, fe2, fe3;
    stars           = true,
    estimator_names = ["OLS", "FE", "FE", "FE"],
    fe_indicator    = "✓",
)
```

## Publication-quality table

Combine all the features — `estimator_names`, `fe_indicator`, `custom_lines`,
and `footnotes` — to replicate a complex academic table in a single call,
with no manual `Cell` construction needed.

```{julia}
#| code-fold: false

# ── Simulated panel data ──────────────────────────────────────────
N, T = 30, 20
panel = DataFrame(
    country = repeat(1:N, inner = T),
    year    = repeat(2001:2000+T, outer = N),
    Δd_HH   = randn(N * T),
    Δd_F    = randn(N * T),
    trend   = repeat(1:T, outer = N) ./ T,
)
panel.Δy = -0.32 .* panel.Δd_HH .- 0.06 .* panel.Δd_F .+
           0.4 .* randn(N * T)
panel.country_cat = categorical(panel.country)
panel.year_cat    = categorical(panel.year)

# ── Four specifications ───────────────────────────────────────────
spec1 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat)))
spec2 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat)))
spec3 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + trend + fe(country_cat)))
spec4 = reg(panel, @formula(Δy ~ Δd_HH + Δd_F + fe(country_cat) + fe(year_cat)))

# ── Build the table ───────────────────────────────────────────────
modelsummary(spec1, spec2, spec3, spec4;
    stars           = true,
    estimator_names = ["OLS", "OLS", "OLS", "OLS"],
    fe_indicator    = "✓",
    labels          = Dict(
        "Δd_HH" => "Δ₃dᴴᴴ",
        "Δd_F"  => "Δ₃dᶠ",
    ),
    regression_statistics = [Nobs, R2],
    custom_lines = [
        "Sample"   => ["Nonoverl.", "Nonoverl.", "Full", "Full"],
    ],
    footnotes = [
        "Standard errors in parentheses.",
        Concat(
            Superscript("***"), " p<0.01, ",
            Superscript("**"),  " p<0.05, ",
            Superscript("*"),   " p<0.1.",
        ),
        " All regressions include country fixed effects.",
    ],
)
```

## Writing to a file

```{julia}
#| eval: false

# LaTeX
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.tex")

# HTML
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.html")

# Typst
modelsummary(m1, m2, m3; stars = true, file = "table.typ")
```
